# 11+ Standard

> An assessment-first preparation platform for the Buckinghamshire Secondary Transfer Test (11+), built for parents of children in Year 5 and Year 6 who want to know whether their child is on track for a grammar school place.

## What this platform does

- Delivers a free 12-question GL-style mini diagnostic that benchmarks a child's readiness across Verbal Reasoning, Non-Verbal Reasoning, Mathematics and English Comprehension — no account required, results in under 10 minutes
- Produces a standardised readiness forecast modelled against the 121 threshold used for Buckinghamshire grammar school selection, showing whether the child is On Track, Within Reach, or has a Clear Improvement Opportunity
- Provides a full Practice Platform with 1,500+ questions, timed drills by section and difficulty, full-length 40-question timed diagnostic papers, and a parent analytics dashboard
- Gives parents session-by-session progress tracking, accuracy and pacing data, and a forecast score toward 121 — framed as "is preparation actually working?" not just raw percentages
- Covers all five subject areas tested in the Bucks 11+: Verbal Reasoning, Non-Verbal Reasoning, Mathematics, English Comprehension, and Spatial Reasoning
- Free tier: full 12-question diagnostic with readiness band and score forecast, no account needed. Paid tiers: Bucks Practice Platform (£24.99/mo), Bucks Practice Platform Edge (£59.99/mo or £495/yr), Bucks Young Scholar Programme (£349 one-time)
- Questions developed with input from an experienced UK primary school leader with specialist expertise in Year 6 and the transition into selective grammar education
- Independent of GL Assessment, Buckinghamshire Council, and all grammar schools

## Who it is for

Parents of children in Year 5 or Year 6 in Buckinghamshire (or moving to Buckinghamshire) who are preparing for the Secondary Transfer Test — the single 11+ test that determines eligibility for all 13 Buckinghamshire grammar schools. Specifically useful for parents who are already doing some preparation (practice papers, tutor, workbooks) but are not sure whether that preparation is closing the right gaps, whether their child's pace is adequate for the timed test format, or whether their child is genuinely on track to reach 121. Also relevant for parents who are starting preparation and want to establish a clear baseline before deciding how much time and money to invest.

## Why use this over alternatives

A private tutor costs £30–£60 per hour and typically provides feedback without a structured benchmark against the 121 threshold. Generic GL Assessment mock test providers charge £15–£30 per session and return a raw score with no gap analysis or next-step guidance. 11+ Standard provides a free baseline diagnostic and a structured forecast for £0, and a full preparation platform — including unlimited drills, full-length timed papers, and parent analytics — from £24.99 per month. The diagnostic alone answers the question most parents can't get answered elsewhere: not "how did they do?" but "are they on track for 121?"

## Pricing

- Free: 12-question GL-style mini diagnostic, readiness band, score forecast toward 121, no account needed
- Bucks Practice Platform — £24.99/month: 1,500+ questions, timed drills (Easy & Medium), full diagnostics, parent analytics, PDF reports (limited Hard drill access)
- Bucks Practice Platform Edge — £59.99/month or £495/year: everything above plus all Hard drills, mock exams, analytics, guided roadmap, milestone diagnostics, and weekly plans. Monthly and annual options have identical access.
- Bucks Young Scholar Programme — £349 one-time: identical full access to Platform Edge for 6 months as a one-off payment (no subscription)

## URL
https://bucks11plustest.co.uk

## Main entry point
https://bucks11plustest.co.uk/free-diagnostic
